title: PeerTube is a free, decentralized and federated video platform
titleZh: PeerTube 是一个免费、去中心化且联邦式的视频平台。
author: doener
relevanceReason: PeerTube 是一个免费、去中心化和联邦制的视频平台，属于开源项目，与科技、开源、云服务相关。
summaryRaw: Website | Join an instance | Create an instance | Chat with us | Donate

Be part of a network of multiple small federated, interoperable video hosting providers. Follow video creators and create videos. No vendor lock-in. All on a platform that is community-owned and ad-free.

Introduction PeerTube is a free, decentralized and federated video platform developed as an alternative to other platforms that centralize our data and attention, such as YouTube, Dailymotion or Vimeo. 🎬 To learn more:
This two-minute video (hosted on PeerTube) explaining what PeerTube is and how it works PeerTube's project homepage, joinpeertube.org Demonstration instances:
summaryZh: PeerTube 是一个免费、去中心化和联邦制的视频平台，旨在替代 YouTube 等中心化视频平台。
该平台由 Framasoft 开发，支持社区所有、无广告，且无供应商锁定。
用户可以通过加入或创建实例，成为多个小型联邦视频托管提供商网络的一部分。
highlightsZh: 去中心化和联邦制架构，避免数据集中化。
开源项目，社区驱动，无广告模式。
支持互操作性，用户可自由加入或创建实例。
提供替代 YouTube 等中心化平台的解决方案。
由非营利组织 Framasoft 开发和维护。

comments.author: djaro
comments.textRawHtml:
As a professional YouTuber, the main issue I instantly see with this is the lack of monetization.

I think people who don't make videos for a living severely underestimate how expensive it is to produce high-quality videos people want to watch. This isn't like writing a tweet or even posting a picture on Instagram. Even a decent 20-minute video can easily take 40 man-hours of high-skilled labor.

I have a pretty small channel (~100K subscribers) with no employees and relatively low upkeep costs (a few hundred dollars a month), and even I could not make this work if I didn't get at least $500-$1,000 per video on average, since it just takes so much time and money.

Most channels with more than a million subscribers are likely founders working 60-80 hour weeks with multiple full-time employees supporting them. You cannot do that in the hopes of viewers donating $5 here and there.

And yes, there are people who make content for free - most of them fail to hit a hundred views per video. And the difference between a million views and a hundred is 10,000x. You cannot create a platform without big users.

I think any real competitor to YouTube nowadays would have to be backed by a big corporation that can pay big creators million-dollar deals to make the switch. Otherwise it's just dead in the water.
comments.textZhHtml:
作为一名专业的 YouTuber，我立即看到的主要问题是缺乏盈利。

我认为那些不以制作视频为生的人严重低估了制作人们想要观看的高质量视频的成本。这与写推文或什至在 Instagram 上发布图片不同。即使是一个像样的 20 分钟视频也很容易需要 40 个工时的高技能劳动力。

我的频道很小（约 10 万订阅者），没有员工，维护成本也相对较低（每月几百美元），如果平均每个视频没有获得至少 500-1,000 美元的收入，即使我也无法完成这项工作，因为它需要太多的时间和金钱。

大多数拥有超过 100 万订阅者的频道的创始人可能每周工作 60 至 80 小时，并有多名全职员工为其提供支持。你不能指望观众到处捐款 5 美元。

是的，有人免费制作内容 - 他们中的大多数人每个视频的观看次数都没有达到 100 次。 100 万次浏览和 100 次浏览之间的差异是 10,000 倍。没有大用户就无法创建一个平台。

我认为，当今 YouTube 的任何真正竞争对手都必须得到一家大公司的支持，该大公司可以向大创作者支付数百万美元的交易来实现转变。不然就死在水里了。

children.author: infamia
children.textRawHtml: You can publish to both and even better your own domain that simply points to your video hosting provider. Long term you want to own your distribution channel as much as possible, while using YouTube as your lead generation tool to drive true believers to your site and premium distribution channel not owned by YouTube. Otherwise, you will always be subject to platform risk via YouTube's whims which has destroyed many content creators. That's the long term winning play IMO and it doesn't preclude tools like FreeTube.
children.textZhHtml: 您可以发布到这两个域，甚至可以发布到您自己的域，只需指向您的视频托管提供商。从长远来看，您希望尽可能多地拥有自己的分销渠道，同时使用 YouTube 作为潜在客户开发工具，以吸引真正的信徒访问您的网站和不属于 YouTube 的优质分销渠道。否则，您将始终面临 YouTube 的突发奇想带来的平台风险，这已经摧毁了许多内容创作者。在我看来，这是长期获胜的策略，并且它并不排除像 FreeTube 这样的工具。

children.author: ndriscoll
children.textRawHtml: It doesn't have to be for your use-case. e.g. KDE has their own instance. It would perhaps be a good fit for MIT to host their OCW videos, or for Khan Academy to host their material, etc. Videos that are meant to help people to better themselves or collaborate fit better on a platform that doesn't try to be a constant sales funnel.
children.textZhHtml: 它不一定适合您的用例。例如KDE 有自己的实例。麻省理工学院托管他们的 OCW 视频，或者可汗学院托管他们的材料等可能是一个不错的选择。旨在帮助人们提高自我或协作的视频更适合一个不试图成为持续销售漏斗的平台。

children.author: paxys
children.textRawHtml: There are 100M+ channels uploading on YouTube regularly and only 2-3M of them are monetized. Not everyone wants to upload videos on the internet with the explicit goal of making money. Professional creators are overall a very tiny minority, and a platform like YouTube will always be better suited for them (your "small" channel with 100K subscribers is actually in the top 0.5-0.1% of YouTube). There is no reason for Peertube to go after this specific demographic.
children.textZhHtml: YouTube 上定期上传的频道数量超过 1 亿个，但其中只有 2-300 万个频道能够获利。并不是每个人都想在互联网上上传视频，其明确的目标是赚钱。总体而言，专业创作者只是极少数，像 YouTube 这样的平台总是更适合他们（拥有 10 万订阅者的“小”频道实际上位于 YouTube 的前 0.5-0.1%）。 Peertube 没有理由去追求这个特定的人群。

children.author: the_cat_kittles
children.textRawHtml: its fine for the genre of video thats just someone narrating while filming with there phone and almost no editing. if someone is doing something interesting, i prefer this to something well produced, its more candid and relatable, and lacks the artifice most projects designed for youtube have
children.textZhHtml: 对于那种只是有人用手机拍摄时进行旁白且几乎没有任何编辑的视频类型来说，这很好。如果有人正在做一些有趣的事情，我更喜欢这个而不是制作精良的东西，它更坦率和相关，并且缺乏大多数为 YouTube 设计的项目所具有的技巧

children.author: deepsun
children.textRawHtml: Honestly I found that the videos I come to Youtube to watch are either personal, non-monetized hobby video, or just a head talking to camera.
children.textZhHtml: 老实说，我发现我来 YouTube 观看的视频要么是个人的、非盈利的爱好视频，要么只是一个头对着镜头说话。

children.author: ktallett
children.textRawHtml: Your issue is assuming that this is trying to replace YouTube for those who wish to try and make money from this. I suspect this is much more closer to a Google videos or YouTube back in the day which was pretty much just random videos, plus lots of conferences on there (which don't get enough views to monetize). This can easily replace that and is something I would support. YouTube hasn't always been monetising and it is good if we have a competitor against it.
children.textZhHtml: 您的问题是假设这正在尝试取代 YouTube，供那些希望尝试从中赚钱的人使用。我怀疑这更接近当时的 Google 视频或 YouTube，它们几乎只是随机视频，加上那里的大量会议（没有足够的观看次数来货币化）。这可以很容易地取代它我会支持的。 YouTube 并不总是以盈利为目的，如果我们能有一个竞争对手来对抗它，那就太好了。

comments.author: CM30
comments.textRawHtml:
It's a promising system, and I'd probably use it over a non-federated video hosting system if I wanted to run a video hosting site of some kind.

Yet it's currently hard to find a real usecase for it, since neither the content you want nor audience is there on PeerTube at the moment. If you're interested in open source software or data privacy you might find something here or there, but topics like gaming, music, sports or movies are very much underserved on the platform at the moment, and get almost no attention from viewers.

For example, I recently did a test search and found a let's play for the Legend of Zelda: The Wind Waker. The videos had something like 3-5 views on PeerTube, and about 10-15 times that on the creator's YouTube channel.

It's the same issue as on Mastodon and Lemmy to be honest, except exaggerated. If the majority of topics aren't well represented on these platforms, then the general public won't use them. And if the general public won't use them, then the creators that would bring the general public over won't use them either.

They need to figure out a way to encourage people outside of the 'hardcore tech nerd raised on Usenet' audience to use these platforms.
comments.textZhHtml:
这是一个很有前途的系统，如果我想运行某种视频托管网站，我可能会在非联合视频托管系统上使用它。

但目前很难找到它的真正用例，因为目前 PeerTube 上既没有您想要的内容，也没有观众。如果您对开源软件或数据隐私感兴趣，您可能会在这里或那里找到一些东西，但目前该平台上的游戏、音乐、体育或电影等主题的服务非常不足，几乎没有得到观众的关注。

例如，我最近做了一个测试搜索，发现了一个让我们玩《塞尔达传说：风之杖》的游戏。这些视频在 PeerTube 上的观看次数约为 3-5 次，在创作者的 YouTube 频道上的观看次数约为 10-15 倍。

老实说，这与 Mastodon 和 Lemmy 的问题相同，只是有些夸张。如果大多数主题在这些平台上没有得到很好的体现，那么公众就不会使用它们。如果公众不会使用它们，那么吸引公众的创作者也不会使用它们。

他们需要找到一种方法来鼓励“在 Usenet 上培养的铁杆技术呆子”受众之外的人使用这些平台。

children.author: WarmWash
children.textRawHtml:
Creators get 60% of youtube's ad revenue from youtube.

What does Peertube pay?

There is your answer. If people want good stuff, there needs to be money flowing to the source of it. The internet desperately needs to shed this "everything good is totally free" mindset, because what is actually manifests as is "I love taking without the requirement of giving".
children.textZhHtml:
创作者能获得YouTube 60%的广告收入。

PeerTube能支付多少呢？

这就是你的答案。如果人们想要优质内容，就必须有资金流向内容的源头。互联网迫切需要摒弃这种“好东西完全免费”的心态，因为这实际上表现出来的就是“我只喜欢索取，不愿付出”。

children.author: paxys
children.textRawHtml: Online video sharing doesn't have to exclusively mean professional "creators" who make content with hollywood-like budgets and expect massive returns. There are 100+ million accounts regularly uploading on Youtube and only around 2-3 million of them are in the partner program. The overwhelming majority get nothing.
children.textZhHtml: 在线视频分享并不一定非得指那些用好莱坞级别预算制作内容并期望获得巨额回报的专业“创作者”。YouTube上有超过1亿个账号在定期上传视频，但其中只有大约200万到300万个加入了合作伙伴计划。绝大多数人什么也得不到。

children.author: zelphirkalt
children.textRawHtml:
The matter of compensation or donation can be handled completely separately. Creators can be supported on other platforms like Liberapay, Patreon, Kofi, and many creators are supported that way.

If we are talking about clickbait and making money from getting unwanted ads in people's faces, no thank you we don't need more of that.
children.textZhHtml:
报酬或捐赠的问题完全可以分开处理。创作者可以通过Liberapay、Patreon、Kofi等其他平台获得支持，很多创作者确实是这样获得支持的。

如果我们谈论的是标题党，以及靠把不受欢迎的广告强塞到人们眼前来赚钱，那谢谢，我们不需要更多这种东西。

children.author: djaro
children.textRawHtml:
About as likely as opening a grocery store where people can pay through voluntary donations.

I'm a professional YouTuber. The problem with a "donation" system is that, unlike something like tweets or even blog posts which are either free or low-cost to produce, high-quality video is really. expensive. to produce. And people just will not pay if they don't have to.

A good 20-minute video can easily cost 40 man-hours of high-skilled labor to produce. That is, a whole week of labor. And that's not counting expensive equipment, software, licenses, etc.

I cannot run a business on people deciding to give me money for nothing out of the goodness of their heart. And I am still a one-person business, imagine having 5 full time employees. Even YouTubers with millions of subscribers and mature audiences with disposable income often struggle to clear like $5K/month on Patreon. Which, for a multi-person business, is simply not enough. Meanwhile, that same creator might be pulling $20K/month through ads and a similar amount through sponsorships.

YouTube is more similar to Netflix and HBO than Twitter or Reddit. Yes, in theory anyone can upload to YouTube, but the majority of content that is actually watched is at this point produced by full-time creators, some of which are solo self-employed while others are at this point running whole media production companies. And those are the people you need to make a service succeed.
children.textZhHtml:
这概率就跟开一家靠顾客自愿捐款的杂货店差不多。

我是一名全职YouTuber。“捐赠”模式的问题在于，不像发推文或写博客那样几乎零成本或低成本，高质量视频的制作成本真的非、常、高。而且只要不是非看不可，大家根本不愿意掏钱。

一个优质的20分钟视频，很容易就要耗费40个工时的高技能劳动。也就是说，整整一周的工作量。这还没算上昂贵的设备、软件、授权费等等。

我不能指望大家凭良心白给我钱来维持公司运营。而且我现在还只是单人工作室，想象一下要是雇5个全职员工会怎样。就算是那些拥有数百万订阅者、受众成熟且有可支配收入的YouTuber，在Patreon上每个月往往也只能勉强赚到5000美元左右。这对于一个多人团队来说，根本不够。与此同时，同一个创作者靠广告每月可能进账2万美元，赞助费也差不多是这个数。

YouTube的模式其实更像Netflix和HBO，而不是Twitter或Reddit。没错，理论上任何人都能往YouTube上传视频，但目前真正有人看的大部分内容，都是由全职创作者制作的，他们有的是独立个体户，有的则已经运营着整个媒体制作公司。而正是这些人，才能让一个平台成功。

children.author: WarmWash
children.textRawHtml:
No one donates money. It doesn't happen. Conversion rates across the board are around 1%. And then of those donations, most are the lowest tier/lowest increment.

It's the most annoying and persistent counterpoint brought up in these discussions, but it has no grounding in reality. The most popular contingent of viewers are ad-supported, close behind are ad-blocking, then the last 5% are your subscribers and donators.
children.textZhHtml:
根本没人捐款。这种事不会发生的。整体转化率也就1%左右。而且在这些捐款中，绝大多数都是最低档位或最低金额。

这是这类讨论中最烦人、最阴魂不散的反驳观点，但它完全脱离现实。观众中占比最大的是看广告的，紧随其后的是用广告拦截的，最后那5%才是你的订阅者和捐款者。

children.author: austinthetaco
children.textRawHtml: that works for people you regularly watch, but what about the people that put out the rare random great video. or a video on a topic when you're trying to fix something. People dont subscribe to those patreons, and thus those kind of creators rely on ad revenue.
children.textZhHtml: 这对那些你经常看的创作者管用，但那些偶尔才出一个神仙视频的创作者呢？或者当你正急着修东西时，刚好刷到一个相关主题的视频。大家不会去订阅这些人的Patreon，所以这类创作者只能依赖广告收入。

children.author: dylan604
children.textRawHtml: Not really sure how people need to be explained this, but for whatever reason, this most basic of information is seemingly skipped over. Even if peertube wants to pay 65%, that's just a bigger percentage of nothing.
children.textZhHtml: 真不明白这还需要怎么跟人解释，但不知为何，这个最基本的信息似乎总是被忽略。即使PeerTube想支付65%的分成，那也不过是零的更大百分比罢了。

children.author: bobbob1921
children.textRawHtml: I agree this is a real issue, however hopefully a solution is that creators will upload their videos to both sites (ie YouTube is their primary but also upload to peertube at the same time) which is pretty easy to do and eventually a site like peer tube might reach that critical mass with enough content
children.textZhHtml: 我同意这确实是个问题，但希望解决方案是创作者将视频同时上传到这两个平台（即以YouTube为主，同时也上传到PeerTube），这操作起来很简单，最终像PeerTube这样的平台可能会凭借足够的内容达到临界规模。

children.author: karmakurtisaani
children.textRawHtml: Lemmy is pretty ok actually. The lack of big user base is more of a feature than a bug.
children.textZhHtml: 其实Lemmy挺不错的。缺乏庞大的用户群与其说是个缺陷，不如说是个特色。

children.author: organsnyder
children.textRawHtml: I've found the same with Mastadon. It has a pre-eternal-September feel to it.
children.textZhHtml: 我在Mastodon上也发现了同样的情况。它给人一种“永恒的九月”到来之前的感觉。

children.author: al_borland
children.textRawHtml:
Has it normalized? When I first tried Lemmy it was mostly communists talking about communism. Then after some Reddit drama it seemed to be a bunch of people complaining about Reddit.

I generally like smaller sites, but those topics weren’t exactly engaging for me.
children.textZhHtml:
现在常态化了吗？我第一次用Lemmy的时候，里面大多是共产主义者在谈论共产主义。后来Reddit出了点抓马事件，感觉又变成了一群人在抱怨Reddit。

我通常比较喜欢小众网站，但那些话题对我来说实在没什么吸引力。
